title: RepLibBuilder: Automated construction, evaluation, and refinement of species-specific repeat libraries
description: RepLibBuilder (RLB) is a two-step pipeline that rescues, standardizes, diagnoses, and condenses transposable element (TE) libraries. It merges public databases (Repbase, Dfam) with de novo predictions (HiTE, EDTA, RepeatModeler2) and applies a dual-engine diagnostic system (TEsorter protein-domain evidence combined with structural heuristics) to produce a high-fidelity, non-redundant library for genome masking and comparative analysis.

creators.name: Xiang, Yunpeng
creators.affiliation: School of Life Science, Southwest University, Chongqing, China
